commits.role: production invariant tests and registry documentation

limits: Canonical JSON records remain the sole source of truth.
The registry is read-only and does not maintain a second instance inventory.
The existing external SELF_MODEL.json schema is preserved.
Decision records remain registry-visible but outside the external SELF_MODEL.json projection.
RegistryRelation output is diagnostic-only and does not enter runtime graph-neighborhood selection.
Suggested IDs are local and non-transactional.
Exclusive creation prevents current-worktree overwrites but does not provide distributed reservation.
The CLI does not invent substantive record content, commit, push, or authorize governance.
No new record types, statuses, or lifecycle semantics are introduced.
Does not alter context selection, provider prompts, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, runtime manifests, or automatic context inclusion.
name: Parametric self-model registry and invariant verification
recommended_next_actions: Use SelfModelRegistry and invariant-based tests for future existing-family record additions.
Use create_self_model_record.py only with complete human-authored payloads and resolve cross-branch ID collisions through Git.
Keep normalized SELF_MODEL.json projection drift enforcement active.
Verify PLAN-20260710-0004 completion through a separate verification and warrant checkpoint.
risks: A future test may lose independence if its expected inventory is derived through the same registry path being tested.
Concurrent branches may receive the same locally suggested ID and must resolve the collision during merge or rebase.
Adding a new record family still requires an explicit ontology and validator change.
summary: Provides a read-only parametric registry over canonical self-model JSON records, registry-backed aggregation, reference and lifecycle metadata, normalized diagnostic relations, projection-drift enforcement, production invariant tests, and a validated collision-safe local record-creation CLI.
